description: Compliance requirements register for the State Application Inventory web app. Tracks all applicable policies, standards, and legal requirements. Each requirement identifies its source, what it mandates, how the product addresses it, and its current status.
note: This register should be reviewed at the start of each development sprint and updated whenever new policies or standards are identified.
complianceNote: Compliance requirements listed are based on Washington State policies and are included as examples. Adopting states should replace these with their own applicable policies, standards, and legal requirements.

requirements.name: Digital Accessibility — WCAG 2.2 AA
requirements.source: State digital accessibility policy (example: USER-01 — WaTech Digital Accessibility Policy)
requirements.mandate: All state agency digital services must meet WCAG 2.2 AA. Deadline and scope vary by state — the Washington State mandate requires compliance by July 1, 2026 for public-facing services and July 1, 2029 for employee-facing tools.
requirements.productResponse: USWDS component library used throughout — built to WCAG 2.2 AA. All custom components must be audited before release.
requirements.testingRequired: Automated accessibility scan (axe or similar) + manual screen reader testing before each release
requirements.deadline: Varies by state — Washington State example: 2026-07-01 for public-facing

requirements.name: IT Portfolio Management — Application Inventory
requirements.source: State IT portfolio policy standard (example: MGMT-01-01-S — Application Standard)
requirements.mandate: All state agencies must maintain an accurate application inventory and submit it as part of the annual certification process.
requirements.productResponse: Core product purpose. Application inventory fields derived from state IT portfolio policy minimum data requirements (Washington State MGMT-01-01-S used as example). Adopting states should map data fields to their own policy standard.
requirements.testingRequired: Validate all required fields from the applicable state policy standard are captured in data model before launch
requirements.deadline: Annual — deadline set by state (Washington State example: September 30)

requirements.name: IT Portfolio Management — Infrastructure Inventory
requirements.source: State IT portfolio policy standard (example: MGMT-01-02-S — Infrastructure Standard)
requirements.mandate: All state agencies must maintain an accurate infrastructure inventory.
requirements.productResponse: Out of scope for v1. Infrastructure inventory to be addressed in a future release.
requirements.deadline: Annual — deadline set by state (Washington State example: September 30)

requirements.name: Statewide IT Portfolio — Legislative Reporting
requirements.source: State IT portfolio legislation (example: RCW 43.105.341 — Information Technology Portfolios)
requirements.mandate: The state IT authority must maintain a statewide IT portfolio that links agency objectives, business plans, and technology. Used for resource allocation and prioritization by state leadership.
requirements.productResponse: Portfolio Intelligence capability (CAP-07) enables cross-agency analysis and reporting to support legislative reporting requirements.
requirements.testingRequired: Verify Portfolio Intelligence exports meet reporting format requirements before first certification cycle
requirements.status: Addressed in CAP-07 — not yet built

requirements.name: Annual Certification Deadline
requirements.source: State IT authority annual certification process (example: WaTech Annual Certification)
requirements.mandate: Agencies must submit application and infrastructure inventories by the state-defined annual deadline.
requirements.productResponse: Annual Certification capability (CAP-05) enforces the certification deadline via configurable business rules. Stale records block submission. Reminder notifications sent at 30 and 7 days prior.
requirements.testingRequired: Test certification deadline enforcement and notification triggers before first certification cycle
requirements.deadline: Annual — deadline set by state (Washington State example: September 30)

requirements.name: Privacy — Personal Information Collection
requirements.source: State privacy and data protection policy (example: WaTech Privacy and Data Protection Policy)
requirements.reference: https://watech.wa.gov/policies (example — replace with your state equivalent)
requirements.mandate: Systems collecting personal information must document data collection practices and comply with state privacy requirements.
requirements.productResponse: App collects user name and email via identity provider. No resident personal information collected. Privacy assessment required before launch to confirm scope.
requirements.testingRequired: Complete state privacy assessment before launch

requirements.name: Security — Identity and Authentication
requirements.source: State security standard for identification and authentication (example: SEC-06-01-S)
requirements.reference: https://watech.wa.gov/policies (example — replace with your state equivalent)
requirements.mandate: State systems must enforce strong identity and authentication controls.
requirements.productResponse: Identity provider SSO with MFA enforced at the identity provider level — not custom built. Role-based access enforced at application level.
requirements.testingRequired: Security review of identity provider configuration and role enforcement before launch

requirements.name: AI Application Identification
requirements.source: State IT authority AI inventory requirement (example: WaTech 2024 Annual Certification — AI Inventory Requirement)
requirements.mandate: Agencies must identify applications using AI and specifically flag applications using Generative AI.
requirements.productResponse: is_ai_enabled and is_generative_ai fields included in Application data model. Required fields in inventory form.
requirements.testingRequired: Verify AI fields are captured and reportable in Portfolio Intelligence view
requirements.deadline: Annual — deadline set by state (Washington State example: September 30)

reviewSchedule.frequency: Each development sprint and before each major release
reviewSchedule.note: New policies or standards identified during development should be added immediately as new COMP entries
